---
title: "3.4.1 Network interface card (NIC)"
subtitle: "Hardware P133"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .reveal .term { color: #cf1f2e; font-weight: 700; }
        .reveal .mark { background: #fff36d; padding: 0 0.08em; }
        .reveal .tight-list li { margin-bottom: 0.24em; }
        .reveal .figure-note { font-size: 0.82em; line-height: 1.28; }
        .reveal .clean-table table { font-size: 0.76em; line-height: 1.1; }
        .reveal .clean-table th, .reveal .clean-table td { padding: 0.24em 0.32em; vertical-align: top; }
        </style>
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 3 Directory / 返回 Unit 3 目录](../../index.html)

## Network Interface Card

### Hardware for network connectivity

A <span class="term">network interface card (NIC)</span> (网络接口卡) is needed to allow a device to connect to a network (such as the internet):

::: {.tight-list}
- It is usually <span class="mark">part of the device hardware</span>
- Contains the <span class="term">Media Access Control (MAC) address</span> generated at the manufacturing stage
:::

## Wireless NIC (WNIC)

### Wireless connectivity via antenna

<span class="term">Wireless network interface cards/controllers (WNICs)</span> are the same as NICs in that they are used to connect devices to the internet or other networks. However, they use <span class="mark">wireless connectivity</span>:

::: {.tight-list}
- Utilise an <span class="mark">antenna</span> to communicate with networks via <span class="mark">microwaves</span>
- Would normally plug into the <span class="mark">USB port</span> or be part of an internal integrated circuit
:::

## NIC and MAC Address

### Permanent hardware identifier

A key feature of every NIC is its built-in identifier:

::: {.tight-list}
- The NIC contains the <span class="mark">MAC address</span> generated at the manufacturing stage
- <span class="mark">If the NIC card is replaced, the MAC address will also change</span>
- This ties the network identity to the physical hardware
:::

## Classroom Check

### Understand NICs and their role

A complete answer should include:

::: {.tight-list}
- that a <span class="term">NIC</span> is needed to <span class="mark">allow a device to connect to a network</span> (such as the internet)
- it is usually <span class="mark">part of the device hardware</span> and contains the <span class="term">MAC address</span> generated at the manufacturing stage
- <span class="term">WNICs</span> use <span class="mark">wireless connectivity</span> utilising an antenna to communicate via microwaves; they plug into the USB port or are part of an internal integrated circuit
- if the NIC card is replaced, the <span class="mark">MAC address will also change</span>
:::

## Bridge

### Next: MAC Addresses

The next deck covers MAC addresses (physical addresses) that NICs use for local network communication.

## End

### Return to the main outline

[← Back to Unit 3 Directory / 返回 Unit 3 目录](../../index.html)
